heldout.missing_axis: Is each finding substantiated by a reproducing counterexample (real defect) or an over-flag? Disposition before rework; reject findings that do not reproduce.

A_ordinary: Verify if findings represent critical security vulnerabilities or logic errors
Assess if findings constitute performance bottlenecks affecting throughput
Evaluate if findings are style violations or non-functional code smells
B_skepticism: Verify if findings are in production-critical paths or dead code
Assess if findings violate explicit security or compliance requirements
Check if findings represent actual exploit vectors or theoretical edge cases
C_lessons: Verify if the reported findings are artifacts of specific test configurations or load conditions rather than intrinsic code flaws.
Assess whether the 'load-bearing tests' adequately cover edge cases and failure modes not captured by standard validation suites.
Determine if the findings represent a deviation from the intended logical specification or merely a stylistic/structural difference.
D_retrieval: Audit finding reproducibility under randomized input conditions
Validation of audit tool's own false-positive rate on known-good code
Impact analysis of proposed changes on existing load-bearing test suite
T1_MF001: Semantic Gap: Load-bearing test coverage vs. real-world adversarial inputs
Audit Validity: Source of findings (formal verification vs. heuristic fuzzing)
Risk Tolerance: Cost of false negatives vs. cost of rework
T2_MFM2artifact: Verify if the reported issues are reproducible under adversarial or edge-case inputs rather than standard load-bearing scenarios
Determine if the code changes are required to satisfy explicit specification constraints or are merely stylistic/preference-based
Assess if the findings represent a security vulnerability or data integrity risk versus a non-functional implementation detail

heldout.missing_axis: Do the supplied tests cover all malformed shapes? The tested surface may be narrower than the claimed property (a fail-open on an untested shape). Attack test-set adequacy.

B_skepticism: Verify coverage of edge cases and boundary conditions not included in the 7 tests
Assess performance and latency under peak load or stress testing
Audit for security vulnerabilities and input validation bypasses
C_lessons: Verify gate behavior under perturbed qubit initialization states (birth mode)
Audit for statistical averaging artifacts that mask localized errors
Map abstract gate failure modes to physical control signal deviations
D_retrieval: Verify label-to-structure mapping integrity against ground truth
Confirm event-driven execution replaces fixed-timing artifacts
Insert paired audit checkpoints for bit-identity verification prior to runtime
T1_MF001: Coverage: Unobserved edge cases in the 7-test suite
Adversarial: Robustness against inputs outside test distribution
Context: Environmental or dependency changes post-audit
T2_MFM2artifact: Edge case coverage beyond the 7 supplied tests
Security and side-effect audit of the implementation
Performance and resource usage under load

heldout.missing_axis: Does the SPECIFIED capability actually work / perform in OUR local config? Docs give no local numbers; measure local behavior — it may fail locally. Specification != local applicability.

A_ordinary: Verify actual latency reduction of model switching with fast resume enabled versus baseline
Audit memory footprint and fragmentation during rapid model load/unload cycles
Measure data consistency and state integrity after resume operations under concurrent load
B_skepticism: Verify actual latency reduction under production load vs. vendor benchmarks
Audit state persistence integrity and corruption risks during resume
Measure cold-start penalty if resume cache is evicted or invalidated
C_lessons: Verify state serialization compatibility across model version upgrades to prevent silent data corruption
Measure actual latency overhead of resume vs. cold start to confirm cost reduction claims
Audit checkpoint integrity and recovery success rates under simulated failure conditions
D_retrieval: verify state serialization completeness against model architecture changes
measure latency overhead of resume vs cold-start across different hardware
audit data integrity and bit-identity of restored tensors after deserialization
T1_MF001: Control Source: Verify if resume state is managed by vendor black-box or exposed for internal validation
State Nature: Audit if resumed state preserves full probabilistic distribution or collapses to deterministic shortcuts
Timing: Measure latency variance between cold start and resume to ensure 'fast' claim holds under load
T2_MFM2artifact: Verify if 'fast resume' preserves full model state fidelity or only partial/quantized state
Measure the actual latency and memory overhead of the resume operation versus a cold start
Check for side-effects such as cache invalidation penalties or consistency errors during state restoration

heldout.missing_axis: The co-serve impossibility is a DECLARED assertion, not measured — measure it first. And the operating-mode choice depends on an unmeasured variable (swap frequency); measure before deciding.

A_ordinary: GPU memory footprint and peak VRAM usage per model
Inference latency and throughput requirements for each model
Batch size compatibility and memory fragmentation risks
B_skepticism: Verify actual VRAM consumption per model including activation memory and optimizer states
Measure GPU utilization and memory bandwidth saturation under concurrent load
Check for kernel launch overhead and context-switch penalties in mixed-mode execution
C_lessons: GPU memory footprint and peak utilization per model
Inference latency and throughput under concurrent load
Batch size compatibility and memory fragmentation risks
D_retrieval: GPU memory footprint and peak utilization under concurrent load
Inference latency distribution and tail-percentile stability
Batching efficiency and throughput degradation when co-located
T1_MF001: State Nature: Deterministic Output vs. Probabilistic Claim
T2_MFM2artifact: GPU Memory Fragmentation and Allocation Overlap
Shared Resource Contention (e.g., NVLink, PCIe Bandwidth)

heldout.missing_axis: Confirm the firing CONDITION mechanism: is 77% a property of the mechanism, or an artifact of the experimental setup (tracking length / accumulation)? Check the construction before treating the metric as a finding.

A_ordinary: Verify sample size and statistical significance of the 77% figure
Check for selection bias in the 'main run' vs 'short smoke run' datasets
Analyze temporal trends to rule out transient anomalies or drift
B_skepticism: Verify if the 25.6% baseline is statistically robust or an artifact of low sample size in the smoke test
Check for selection bias in the 'main run' cohort (e.g., did only stable users trigger the full firing logic?)
Audit the definition of 'one-directional' to ensure it isn't conflated with 'successful' vs 'failed' attempts
C_lessons: Verify if the 77% statistic is an artifact of the 'one-directional' definition or binning thresholds.
Check for temporal or spatial averaging effects that mask multi-directional or oscillatory firing patterns.
Confirm the sensor calibration and noise floor to ensure 'one-directional' is not a detection limit artifact.
T1_MF001: State Nature: Deterministic Output vs. Probabilistic Claim
